@graph.name: AND
@graph.semantics: fire when all required inputs arrive (max-arrival join)

@graph.name: OR
@graph.semantics: fire on earliest arriving input, no synchronization

@graph.name: FIRST
@graph.semantics: first input to arrive wins, others ignored

@graph.name: THRESHOLD
@graph.semantics: fire when n of m inputs arrive (n-th earliest)

@graph.name: breaker
@graph.semantics: trips_on a condition, self-recovers after recovery_ms

@graph.name: sentinel
@graph.semantics: guards a protected gate; issues/verifies auth tokens

@graph.name: outcome-based routing
@graph.semantics: distinct gate outcomes route to distinct downstream synapses

@graph.name: synapse
@graph.semantics: a typed edge carrying a pulse from one gate outcome to another gate input

@graph.name: refractory period
@graph.semantics: recovery_ms suppression after firing; no WCP counterpart

@graph.name: input window
@graph.semantics: input_timeout_ms temporal scoping of a join; no WCP counterpart

@graph.name: probabilistic outcome
@graph.semantics: outcomes carry probabilities for twin sampling; no WCP counterpart

@graph.name: latency budget
@graph.semantics: p50/p95 timing contract on a gate; WCP is untimed

@graph.name: feedback synapse
@graph.semantics: a synapse forming a cycle in the flow graph

@graph.name: recursive gate internals
@graph.semantics: a gate's internals reuse the flow composition format (GA)

@graph.name: Sequence
@graph.semantics: an activity enabled after completion of a preceding one

@graph.name: Parallel Split
@graph.semantics: one thread splits into concurrent threads (AND-split)
@graph.opisGap: fan-out via multiple outgoing synapses

@graph.name: Synchronization
@graph.semantics: AND-join: enable output after all incoming branches complete

@graph.name: Exclusive Choice
@graph.semantics: XOR-split: exactly one outgoing branch chosen by condition

@graph.name: Simple Merge
@graph.semantics: XOR-join: enable output each time any incoming branch completes

@graph.name: Multi-Choice
@graph.semantics: OR-split: one-to-all outgoing branches chosen by conditions
@graph.opisGap: Opis outcomes are mutually exclusive per firing; simultaneous multi-branch activation not modeled

@graph.name: Structured Synchronizing Merge
@graph.semantics: merge exactly the branches a paired Multi-Choice activated
@graph.opisGap: run-determined arrival count; Opis AND expects a statically-declared bundle

@graph.name: Multi-Merge
@graph.semantics: converge without sync; fire once per incoming completion
@graph.opisGap: per-branch repeated firing is a pulse phenomenon in Opis, not a distinct join type

@graph.name: Structured Discriminator
@graph.semantics: first of m to arrive fires output, rest ignored until reset

@graph.name: Blocking Discriminator
@graph.semantics: discriminator that blocks re-triggering per branch across cases
@graph.opisGap: cross-case blocking needs refractory (recovery_ms) to express

@graph.name: Cancelling Discriminator
@graph.semantics: first arrival fires output and cancels remaining branches
@graph.opisGap: active cancellation of losing branches has no Opis primitive (only breaker trip is close)

@graph.name: Structured Partial Join
@graph.semantics: n-out-of-m join: fire when n of m complete, rest ignored

@graph.name: Blocking Partial Join
@graph.semantics: n-of-m join with per-branch blocking across cases
@graph.opisGap: cross-case blocking needs refractory

@graph.name: Cancelling Partial Join
@graph.semantics: n-of-m join that cancels remaining branches once n complete
@graph.opisGap: active cancellation side-effect has no Opis primitive

@graph.name: Generalised AND-Join
@graph.semantics: AND-join in unstructured/concurrent regions, order-independent
@graph.opisGap: matches proof.py AND-join fixed point over general graphs

@graph.name: Local Synchronizing Merge
@graph.semantics: synchronizing merge decided from local acyclic info
@graph.opisGap: corresponds to Opis local reachability analysis, not a gate logic value per se

@graph.name: General Synchronizing Merge
@graph.semantics: synchronizing merge over cyclic topology needing global lookahead
@graph.opisGap: cyclic AND-join is the hard case for the proof.py fixed point; relates to opis:c_feedback

@graph.name: Thread Merge
@graph.semantics: a nominated number of threads on one branch merge
@graph.opisGap: same-type pulse aggregation; Opis joins are over distinct input types

@graph.name: Thread Split
@graph.semantics: one thread on a branch splits into nominated threads
@graph.opisGap: emitting multiple same-type pulses

@graph.name: Multiple Instances without Synchronization
@graph.semantics: spawn independent instances, no later sync
@graph.opisGap: fan-out of independent pulses

@graph.name: MI with Design-Time Knowledge
@graph.semantics: fixed instance count known at design time
@graph.opisGap: static fan-out declared in flow

@graph.name: MI with Run-Time Knowledge
@graph.semantics: instance count known at runtime before creation
@graph.opisGap: data-determined fan-out; count lives in CA payload layer, not the flow graph

@graph.name: MI without Run-Time Knowledge
@graph.semantics: dynamic instances, total unknown until last completes
@graph.opisGap: Opis flows are statically typed; dynamic instance counts unsupported

@graph.name: Static Partial Join for MI
@graph.semantics: of n known instances proceed once m complete
@graph.opisGap: THRESHOLD over instances of one activity, not distinct inputs

@graph.name: Cancelling Partial Join for MI
@graph.semantics: as WCP34 then cancel remaining instances
@graph.opisGap: cancellation side-effect unsupported

@graph.name: Dynamic Partial Join for MI
@graph.semantics: partial join with dynamic instance count
@graph.opisGap: dynamic instances unsupported

@graph.name: Deferred Choice
@graph.semantics: branch chosen by which external event occurs first (race)
@graph.opisGap: environment-driven race; relates to sentinel/external triggers, needs external-locus FIRST

@graph.name: Interleaved Parallel Routing
@graph.semantics: activities run in unknown order but never simultaneously
@graph.opisGap: mutual-exclusion / partial-order serialization has no Opis primitive

@graph.name: Milestone
@graph.semantics: activity enabled only while process is in a specific state
@graph.opisGap: state-window gating; also relates to breaker recovery and opis:c_window

@graph.name: Critical Section
@graph.semantics: prevent concurrent execution of designated regions
@graph.opisGap: mutual exclusion over a shared downstream; throttle/regulator is closest

@graph.name: Interleaved Routing
@graph.semantics: group of activities executed sequentially in any order
@graph.opisGap: serialization constraint unsupported

@graph.name: Cancel Task
@graph.semantics: withdraw an enabled/running activity instance
@graph.opisGap: breaker trip suppresses a gate; not an explicit external cancel signal

@graph.name: Cancel Case
@graph.semantics: remove an entire process instance
@graph.opisGap: aborting a whole flow run; breaker cascade is closest

@graph.name: Cancel Region
@graph.semantics: withdraw a defined set of activities together
@graph.opisGap: breaker tripping a downstream region

@graph.name: Cancel Multiple Instance Activity
@graph.semantics: withdraw all instances of an MI activity
@graph.opisGap: cancellation over dynamic fan-out unsupported

@graph.name: Complete Multiple Instance Activity
@graph.semantics: force an MI activity to complete early
@graph.opisGap: forced early completion; THRESHOLD is closest but no explicit force signal

@graph.name: Arbitrary Cycles
@graph.semantics: unstructured loops with multiple entry/exit points
@graph.opisGap: twin handles cycles via fixed-point iteration

@graph.name: Structured Loop
@graph.semantics: single entry/exit loop with pre- or post-test
@graph.opisGap: loop condition is implicit in outcome routing, not a declared test

@graph.name: Recursion
@graph.semantics: an activity invokes itself directly or indirectly
@graph.opisGap: GA gate internals reuse the composition format recursively

@graph.name: Implicit Termination
@graph.semantics: terminate when nothing is enabled or running
@graph.opisGap: proof.py drain reaching a fixed point is the analog; not a gate construct

@graph.name: Explicit Termination
@graph.semantics: terminate when a designated end node is reached
@graph.opisGap: a terminal locus/sink; flows terminate at loci, not a dedicated end node

@graph.name: Transient Trigger
@graph.semantics: trigger signal lost if activity not ready (no buffering)
@graph.opisGap: fire-and-forget event dropped past input_timeout_ms; event slot type

@graph.name: Persistent Trigger
@graph.semantics: trigger signal retained until activity is ready
@graph.opisGap: buffered/queued durable input; no explicit queue primitive in gate logic (lives in gate internals)
